office_does: One of two House members for LD 47 (Kent, Auburn, Covington, Maple Valley area); writes and votes on state law and the state budgets.
race_blurb: A rematch of the 2024 general election: Democratic incumbent Chris Stearns, the House Speaker Pro Tempore, again faces Republican Ted Cooke, a party-organization activist and software professional without prior elected office. Both advance to the general.

candidates.summary: Incumbent state representative and House Speaker Pro Tempore, a Navajo attorney with roughly 30 years working on Native American rights and multiple bills signed into law.
highlights: Bills signed into law include HB 1461 (clean energy infrastructure), a tribal apprenticeship bill, and HB 1681 doubling problem-gambling funds [S3]
Describes reproductive and gender-affirming care as human rights [S1][S2]
Speaker Pro Tempore with prior Auburn City Council service and a Clinton-era federal appointment; broad labor and Democratic endorsement slate [S1][S2]

experience.basis: Incumbent with House leadership roles, bills signed into law, and prior council, commission, and federal service.

housing.basis: Cites expanding affordable housing through state programs as a delivered result and continued priority.

spending.basis: Runs on delivered state investments in affordable housing, behavioral health, childcare, and nutrition.

climate.basis: Sponsored HB 1461 on clean energy infrastructure, signed into law; supports clean-energy job expansion.

social.basis: Describes reproductive and gender-affirming care as human rights, repeated across pamphlet and campaign site.

safety.basis: Prioritizes behavioral-health investment; served on the King County Mental Illness and Drug Dependency Committee.

tech.basis: Sponsored HB 1221 on data privacy and security, which passed the Senate.

candidates.summary: Republican challenger and senior software tester with King County GOP organizational experience; he lost to Stearns in the 2024 general and runs on tax elimination, state audits, and parental rights.
highlights: Would freeze all new taxes, eliminate taxes on income, property, and fuel, and order audits of every state office and program [S1]
Would replace DEI with merit in government hiring, 'uphold the biological definition of gender,' and restore 'full parental rights in education' [S1][S2]
Prioritizes 'starter homes with yards' over high-density apartments he characterizes as forced on the district by Olympia [S1]

experience.basis: No prior elected public office; runs as an outsider against government overreach after losing to the same incumbent in 2024.

taxes.basis: Would freeze all new taxes and eliminate existing taxes on income, property, and fuel.

spending.basis: Would order audits of every state office and program, cut waste, and end commissions he characterizes as unaccountable.

housing.basis: Prioritizes market single-family 'starter homes with yards' and opposes state-driven density; no tenant-protection or public-housing agenda.

safety.basis: Names public safety a top priority and supports funding police to secure neighborhoods, parks, and schools.

social.basis: Would remove DEI from government hiring, 'uphold the biological definition of gender,' and restore 'full parental rights in education.'

local-control.basis: Characterizes density as forced on the district by Olympia and describes state government as overreaching in regulation and mandates.
